meta.note: 口语题库(占位版)。后续可从剑桥雅思真题 Speaking Part 或题库网站扩充。所有题干为通用类型,与真机考话题相同分布。

questions: What do you do? Do you work or study?
Why did you choose that subject / job?
What do you enjoy most about it?
What are you future plans in this field?
Where is your hometown?
Do you like your hometown? Why?
How has your hometown changed in recent years?
Would you recommend visitors to go there?
What do you usually do in your free time?
When did you start this hobby?
Do you prefer doing it alone or with others?
Do you think hobbies are important for people? Why?
What kind of food do you like?
Do you cook at home? Why or why not?
Is fast food popular in your country?
Has your diet changed over the last few years?

part2.topic: A place you would like to visit
part2.cue:
You should say:
- where it is
- how you first heard about it
- what you would do there
- and explain why you would like to visit this place.

You should say:
- what the skill is
- when and how you learned it
- who taught you (if anyone)
- and explain why this skill is useful for you.

You should say:
- what the decision was
- when you made it
- why you made this decision
- and explain how this decision has affected your life.

part2.topic: An old thing your family has kept for a long time
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what it is
- who it belongs to
- how long your family has had it
- and explain why it is important to your family.

questions: Why do young people and old people travel for different reasons?
How has the way people travel changed in the last 30 years?
Do you think travelling is important for personal development? Why?
Some people say international tourism does more harm than good — do you agree?
Which skills do you think today's children should learn at school?
Is it easier to learn new skills when you are young? Why?
How has the internet changed the way people learn new skills?
Do you think traditional skills like handwriting or cooking are still important today?
Do you think young people are good at making important decisions?
Should parents make big decisions for their children? Why or why not?
How do people usually deal with regret after a bad decision?
Is it better to make decisions quickly or take a lot of time?
